title: Apply for an EUR.1 movement certificate
description: Get an EUR.1 certificate to claim preferential treatment when exporting to the European Union.
summary: An EUR.1 movement certificate proves that goods you are exporting to the European Union (or another country with a similar trade agreement) qualify for preferential customs treatment. Without it, your goods face full duty on arrival.

Customs and Excise issues EUR.1 certificates in Barbados. You complete the form for each shipment and provide documents that show the goods meet the rules of origin under the relevant trade agreement.
The certificate covers a specific shipment. The next shipment needs its own certificate – there is no rolling version of EUR.1 for multiple shipments.

Some exporters can become ‘approved exporters’, which lets them self-certify rather than apply for a new EUR.1 every time. That is a separate process – ask Customs and Excise about it if you export frequently.

items: the exporter’s and consignee’s name, full address and country
the country, group of countries or territory of origin
the country, group of countries or territory of destination
marks and numbers, description of the goods, packages and HS tariff code
invoice number and the basis for claiming origin

items.name: Bill of lading or airway bill
Supplier’s declaration or proof of origin
items.note: Showing that the goods meet the rules of origin.

fees: Confirm fees with the Customs and Excise Department.
how_long: Certificates are usually issued within 3 to 5 working days.

options.heading: In person
content: Take the completed EUR.1 movement certificate to the Customs and Excise Department in Bridgetown.

contact.address:
Customs House, Bridge Street
Bridgetown, St. Michael

assumptions: ‘Certificates are usually issued within 3 to 5 working days’ is an estimate.
‘Approved exporters can self-certify’ is a general EU trade-agreement provision; confirm the current Barbadian implementation.
Customs and Excise contact details (address, phone, email, hours) are illustrative and should be confirmed.
